items.question: What three object types does a typical MLflow or W&B run record?
items.answer: Parameters (static config like learning rate and seed), metrics (numeric scores often logged per step/epoch), and artifacts (files such as model weights, plots, and configs) — all keyed by a unique run ID.

items.question: How do you log a hyperparameter vs a validation score in MLflow?
items.answer: Use mlflow.log_param("learning_rate", 0.01) once for static config; use mlflow.log_metric("val_f1", score, step=epoch) for numeric results that change during training. Params are string key-value; metrics support time series via step.

items.question: What is the W&B equivalent of logging hyperparameters?
items.answer: Pass config to wandb.init(config={...}) or call wandb.config.update({"learning_rate": 0.01, "seed": 42}) at run start. Config appears in the W&B UI Config tab and is included when comparing runs.

items.question: Why must metric key names be consistent across runs before comparison?
items.answer: MLflow and W&B comparison tables pivot on metric and param keys. If one run logs val_auc and another logs validation_auc, sort and filter by val_auc silently excludes or misranks runs — standardize keys in a shared logging helper.

items.question: MLflow vs W&B — one strength of each for beginners?
items.answer: MLflow: local-first open-source tracking with simple mlruns storage and strong Model Registry integration. W&B: cloud project dashboards with live metric streaming and rich interactive run comparison — strong for team visibility during long training jobs.
